title: senecio jacobaea subsp. lycopifolius
intro: Senecio jacobaea subsp. lycopifolius, also known as ragwort, is a subspecies of the ragwort plant, which is a biennial or perennial plant in the sunflower family. The plant contains pyrrolizidine alkaloids, which have been found to have medicinal properties and are used in herbal preparations for their potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Senecio jacobaea subsp. lycopifolius include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve liver function, and act as an antioxidant, although its use should be limited due to potential toxicity. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and infusions, are used to treat a range of health issues, but should be prepared with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
